__instructions: Fill every field below according to the user request and available log listing.
Return ONLY the populated JSON object — no markdown, no explanation.
Remove this '__instructions' key from your output.
incident_time: compact YYYYMMDDHHmm timestamp of the incident extracted from the user query (e.g. 202604010930). Use null if no specific time was mentioned.
proposed_keywords: only exact words present in the user query OR literal strings from source-code logger/exception statements.

proposed_logs: keys MUST be exact 'prefix' values taken from the runtime Available Log Listing — never invent log names. Each value is an object whose keys are relevant category labels (e.g. 'Strategy', 'error') and whose values are {start, end} timestamp objects in 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.mmm' format (e.g. "2026-04-01 08:00:00.000"). Use null for unknown timestamps.
extracted_identifiers: raw sensitive tokens from the query (ISINs, trade IDs, order IDs, etc.).
incident_time: <<YYYYMMDDHHmm compact timestamp from the query, or null>>
proposed_keywords: <<exact word from user query or source-code log/exception text>>
